CivilisationOS · Domain 114 · Cluster 5 — Economics & Business · Region: AFRICA · Currency: MZN
BANCO DE MOÇAMBIQUE — MOZAMBIQUE CANARY
ROVUMA BASIN 100 TCF · TOTAL $20B LNG SUSPENDED · INSURGENCY ACTIVE
Issued: 2026-07-06  |  Domain: bm_mozambique_canary  |  Risk Index: MZRI  |  Q3 2026 Intelligence Brief
0.4250
MZRI Score
WATCH
MZRI — Mozambique Risk Index
0.0 NORMAL 0.30 WATCH 0.60 ELEVATED 0.80 CRITICAL 1.0
Formula: KC_01×0.35 + KC_02×0.25 + KC_03×0.20 + KC_04×0.15 + KC_05×0.05
THE CANARY IS WATCHING
Issued: 2026-07-06
⚠
KC_01 SINGLE TRIGGER — LNG ATTACK / INSURGENCY
Ansar al-Sunna (ISIS-linked) insurgency active in Cabo Delgado since 2017. Palma attack (Mar 2021): 100+ killed. Afungi LNG terminal threat forced TotalEnergies $20B suspension. If KC_01 score ≥ 0.85, MZRI is forced to CRITICAL regardless of other KCs. Current KC_01: 0.48 WATCH. SADC mission deployed; insurgency reduced but not eliminated.

Key Context — Mozambique Rovuma Basin

Rovuma Basin Reserves
~100 TCF
World's 4th largest natural gas reserves
TotalEnergies Mozambique LNG
SUSPENDED
$20B project, 12.88 MTPA. Suspended Apr 2021 after Palma attack
Eni Coral Sul FLNG
OPERATIONAL
First cargo Jun 2022. ~1 MTPA. Floating = more secure
ExxonMobil Rovuma LNG
DELAYED
$30B project. Awaiting Total resumption signal. 2028+ start
Insurgency Group
Ansar al-Sunna
ISIS-linked ("Al-Shabaab" local). Active Cabo Delgado since 2017
Tuna Bond Scandal
$2B Hidden Debt
2013-14 hidden state loans. IMF programme suspended 2016. Debt-to-GDP ~100%
Cyclone Idai (2019)
600 Dead
$3B damage. Beira direct hit. Worst in southern Africa history (at time)
Cyclone Freddy (2023)
Record Energy
Most energetic tropical cyclone ever recorded globally

Kill Condition Breakdown — MZRI Components

KC ID Kill Condition Score Zone Weight Contribution Notes
KC_01 SINGLE TRIGGER LNG Project Attack / Insurgency 0.4800 WATCH 0.35 0.1680 Ansar al-Sunna (ISIS-linked) insurgency Cabo Delgado; Palma 2021 (100+ killed); Afungi terminal. SINGLE TRIGGER: score ≥ 0.85 → MZRI CRITICAL
KC_02 Total SE / Eni LNG Suspension 0.4500 WATCH 0.25 0.1125 TotalEnergies $20B Mozambique LNG suspended Apr 2021; Eni Coral Sul FLNG operational; ExxonMobil $30B Rovuma LNG delayed
KC_03 Debt Sustainability / Tuna Bond Crisis 0.3800 WATCH 0.20 0.0760 $2B hidden state loans 2013-14 (Tuna Bonds); IMF programme suspended 2016; debt-to-GDP ~100%; IMF ECF fragile
KC_04 Cyclone / Climate Catastrophe 0.2800 NORMAL 0.15 0.0420 Idai (2019): 600 dead, $3B damage; Freddy (2023): most energetic cyclone ever recorded; Kenneth (2019); seasonal corridor risk
KC_05 MZN Currency Stability 0.3000 WATCH 0.05 0.0150 Mozambican metical highly volatile vs USD; LNG revenues (when flowing) provide FX stability; IMF pressure
TOTAL MZRI: 0.4250 WATCH   THE CANARY IS WATCHING

MZRI Formula Reference

MZRI = KC_01×0.35 + KC_02×0.25 + KC_03×0.20 + KC_04×0.15 + KC_05×0.05
SINGLE TRIGGER: IF KC_01 ≥ 0.85 THEN MZRI ← CRITICAL (score forced ≥ 0.80)
Channel composites:
  lng_supply_chain = KC01×0.50 + KC02×0.40 + KC04×0.10
  energy_security = KC02×0.60 + KC01×0.40
  em_africa = KC03×0.50 + KC01×0.30 + KC05×0.20
  safe_haven = KC01×0.70 + KC04×0.30
Zones: NORMAL <0.30 | WATCH 0.30-0.60 | ELEVATED 0.60-0.80 | CRITICAL ≥0.80

Supply Chain Risk Intelligence
Chokepoint · Freight Rate · Critical Minerals · Nearshoring · Logistics
SCRI  0.1400  NORMAL

Banco de Moçambique operates a frontier, structurally shallow supply chain, heavily dependent on a small number of entry points with minimal alternative routing — any chokepoint disruption has outsized domestic impact. Maritime chokepoint exposure is concentrated across: Cape of Good Hope (moderate dependency, weight 0.20).

Critical import dependencies include refined fuels, food commodities, industrial machinery. Energy import dependency means commodity price spikes and Middle East shipping disruptions transmit directly to domestic inflation and current account pressure.

Primary exports — LNG, coal, aluminum, gas — are sensitive to global freight rate movements and trade route disruptions.

SCRI 0.1400 NORMAL — Supply chain channels are operating within baseline parameters. No structural disruption signals active at this time.

Watch signals: Cape of Good Hope closure or capacity restriction → immediate freight rate spike  |  SCFI/WCI/BDI freight index spike above 2-standard-deviation threshold → freight stress trigger

Kill Condition Breakdown
Kill Condition Weight Score Zone
KC_SC01 Chokepoint Disruption30%0.1300NORMAL
KC_SC02 Freight Rate Stress25%0.0500NORMAL
KC_SC03 Critical Minerals20%0.2000NORMAL
KC_SC04 Nearshoring / FDI Shift15%0.0500NORMAL
KC_SC05 Logistics Infrastructure10%0.1000NORMAL

Real Estate Risk Intelligence
Property Price · Mortgage Stress · Commercial RE · Developer Debt · REIT
RERI  0.0500  NORMAL

Banco de Moçambique is classified as a frontier-market real estate market. Primary stress vector is residential affordability. Commercial real estate exposure is limited CRE concentration risk.

As a frontier market, Mozambique real estate operates with limited pricing transparency, thin transaction volumes, and minimal mortgage market depth. Property rights frameworks may be underdeveloped, creating additional legal risk for international investors.

No active developer stress flagged. Property developers in Mozambique are not currently assessed as in distress at a systemic level.

RERI 0.0500 NORMAL — Property market stable at structural baseline. No systemic stress indicators active.

Watch signals: Property price index 3-month decline exceeding 2% → KC_RE01 trigger  |  Capital outflow episode → property market freeze and liquidity withdrawal

Kill Condition Weight Score Zone

Insurance Risk Intelligence
Natural Catastrophe · Reinsurance · Property Withdrawal · Financial Guaranty · Climate
INRI  0.4200  WATCH

Banco de Moçambique faces 3 identified primary natural catastrophe perils: Hurricane / Typhoon, Flood, Drought Heatwave. High-severity events (Hurricane / Typhoon) are present — these carry the highest economic loss potential and are most likely to cause reinsurance market dislocations.

The insurance market in Mozambique is minimal, with negligible domestic insurance infrastructure — catastrophic events rely almost entirely on international reinsurance or sovereign guarantees. Reinsurance profile: high reinsurance dependency — most catastrophic risk ceded offshore.

Climate risk classification: Tier 3 — high climate vulnerability; physical and transition risks are material and accelerating. Physical climate risks — including intensifying storm frequency, sea level rise, and heat stress on agriculture — are expected to materially increase insured losses over the medium term, potentially triggering insurer withdrawal from high-risk zones and creating insurance coverage gaps.

INRI 0.4200 WATCH — Insurance market faces structural vulnerabilities. Nat-cat exposure and/or reinsurance dependency requires monitoring. A major catastrophic event could expose coverage gaps rapidly.

Watch signals: Major Hurricane / Typhoon event → test of reinsurance capacity and coverage adequacy  |  Global reinsurance market hardening (Jan or July renewals) → premium spike or capacity withdrawal  |  Insurer non-renewal notices in high-risk zones → coverage gap emergence  |  Sovereign disaster declaration without insurance backstop → fiscal cliff  |  Cat bond spread widening → forward signal of reinsurance market stress

Kill Condition Breakdown
Kill Condition Weight Score Zone

KC_IN02 Reinsurance Market25%0.3200WATCH
KC_IN03 Property Ins. Withdrawal20%0.3500WATCH

KC_IN05 Climate Risk Dislocation8%0.2300NORMAL

Insurance Risk Intelligence
Natural Catastrophe · Reinsurance · Property Withdrawal · Financial Guaranty · Climate
INRI  0.4200  WATCH

Banco de Moçambique faces 3 identified primary natural catastrophe perils: Hurricane / Typhoon, Flood, Drought Heatwave. High-severity events (Hurricane / Typhoon) are present — these carry the highest economic loss potential and are most likely to cause reinsurance market dislocations.

The insurance market in Mozambique is minimal, with negligible domestic insurance infrastructure — catastrophic events rely almost entirely on international reinsurance or sovereign guarantees. Reinsurance profile: high reinsurance dependency — most catastrophic risk ceded offshore.

Climate risk classification: Tier 3 — high climate vulnerability; physical and transition risks are material and accelerating. Physical climate risks — including intensifying storm frequency, sea level rise, and heat stress on agriculture — are expected to materially increase insured losses over the medium term, potentially triggering insurer withdrawal from high-risk zones and creating insurance coverage gaps.

INRI 0.4200 WATCH — Insurance market faces structural vulnerabilities. Nat-cat exposure and/or reinsurance dependency requires monitoring. A major catastrophic event could expose coverage gaps rapidly.

Watch signals: Major Hurricane / Typhoon event → test of reinsurance capacity and coverage adequacy  |  Global reinsurance market hardening (Jan or July renewals) → premium spike or capacity withdrawal  |  Insurer non-renewal notices in high-risk zones → coverage gap emergence  |  Sovereign disaster declaration without insurance backstop → fiscal cliff  |  Cat bond spread widening → forward signal of reinsurance market stress

Kill Condition Breakdown
Kill Condition Weight Score Zone
KC_IN01 Natural Catastrophe35%0.3000WATCH
KC_IN02 Reinsurance Market25%0.3200WATCH
KC_IN03 Property Ins. Withdrawal20%0.3500WATCH
KC_IN04 Financial Guaranty12%0.1300NORMAL
KC_IN05 Climate Risk Dislocation8%0.2300NORMAL

Supply Chain Risk Intelligence
Chokepoint · Freight Rate · Critical Minerals · Nearshoring · Logistics
SCRI  0.1400  NORMAL

Banco de Moçambique operates a frontier, structurally shallow supply chain, heavily dependent on a small number of entry points with minimal alternative routing — any chokepoint disruption has outsized domestic impact. Maritime chokepoint exposure is concentrated across: Cape of Good Hope (moderate dependency, weight 0.20).

Critical import dependencies include refined fuels, food commodities, industrial machinery. Energy import dependency means commodity price spikes and Middle East shipping disruptions transmit directly to domestic inflation and current account pressure.

Primary exports — LNG, coal, aluminum, gas — are sensitive to global freight rate movements and trade route disruptions.

SCRI 0.1400 NORMAL — Supply chain channels are operating within baseline parameters. No structural disruption signals active at this time.

Watch signals: Cape of Good Hope closure or capacity restriction → immediate freight rate spike  |  SCFI/WCI/BDI freight index spike above 2-standard-deviation threshold → freight stress trigger

Kill Condition Breakdown
Kill Condition Weight Score Zone
KC_SC01 Chokepoint Disruption30%0.1300NORMAL
KC_SC02 Freight Rate Stress25%0.0500NORMAL
KC_SC03 Critical Minerals20%0.2000NORMAL
KC_SC04 Nearshoring / FDI Shift15%0.0500NORMAL
KC_SC05 Logistics Infrastructure10%0.1000NORMAL
ⓘ Structural config baseline — scores derived from sovereign metadata; live article analysis raises these floors.  |  Region: Africa | Supply depth: frontier | Chokepoints: Cape of Good Hope  |  Issued: 2026-07-06 12:40 UTC
Real Estate Risk Intelligence
Property Price · Mortgage Stress · Commercial RE · Developer Debt · REIT
RERI  0.0500  NORMAL

Banco de Moçambique is classified as a frontier-market real estate market. Primary stress vector is residential affordability. Commercial real estate exposure is limited CRE concentration risk.

As a frontier market, Mozambique real estate operates with limited pricing transparency, thin transaction volumes, and minimal mortgage market depth. Property rights frameworks may be underdeveloped, creating additional legal risk for international investors.

No active developer stress flagged. Property developers in Mozambique are not currently assessed as in distress at a systemic level.

RERI 0.0500 NORMAL — Property market stable at structural baseline. No systemic stress indicators active.

Watch signals: Property price index 3-month decline exceeding 2% → KC_RE01 trigger  |  Capital outflow episode → property market freeze and liquidity withdrawal

Kill Condition Weight Score Zone

Real Estate Risk Intelligence
Property Price · Mortgage Stress · Commercial RE · Developer Debt · REIT
RERI  0.0500  NORMAL

Banco de Moçambique is classified as a frontier-market real estate market. Primary stress vector is residential affordability. Commercial real estate exposure is limited CRE concentration risk.

As a frontier market, Mozambique real estate operates with limited pricing transparency, thin transaction volumes, and minimal mortgage market depth. Property rights frameworks may be underdeveloped, creating additional legal risk for international investors.

No active developer stress flagged. Property developers in Mozambique are not currently assessed as in distress at a systemic level.

RERI 0.0500 NORMAL — Property market stable at structural baseline. No systemic stress indicators active.

Watch signals: Property price index 3-month decline exceeding 2% → KC_RE01 trigger  |  Capital outflow episode → property market freeze and liquidity withdrawal

Insurance Risk Intelligence
Natural Catastrophe · Reinsurance · Property Withdrawal · Financial Guaranty · Climate
INRI  0.4200  WATCH

Banco de Moçambique faces 3 identified primary natural catastrophe perils: Hurricane / Typhoon, Flood, Drought Heatwave. High-severity events (Hurricane / Typhoon) are present — these carry the highest economic loss potential and are most likely to cause reinsurance market dislocations.

The insurance market in Mozambique is minimal, with negligible domestic insurance infrastructure — catastrophic events rely almost entirely on international reinsurance or sovereign guarantees. Reinsurance profile: high reinsurance dependency — most catastrophic risk ceded offshore.

Climate risk classification: Tier 3 — high climate vulnerability; physical and transition risks are material and accelerating. Physical climate risks — including intensifying storm frequency, sea level rise, and heat stress on agriculture — are expected to materially increase insured losses over the medium term, potentially triggering insurer withdrawal from high-risk zones and creating insurance coverage gaps.

INRI 0.4200 WATCH — Insurance market faces structural vulnerabilities. Nat-cat exposure and/or reinsurance dependency requires monitoring. A major catastrophic event could expose coverage gaps rapidly.

Watch signals: Major Hurricane / Typhoon event → test of reinsurance capacity and coverage adequacy  |  Global reinsurance market hardening (Jan or July renewals) → premium spike or capacity withdrawal  |  Insurer non-renewal notices in high-risk zones → coverage gap emergence  |  Sovereign disaster declaration without insurance backstop → fiscal cliff  |  Cat bond spread widening → forward signal of reinsurance market stress
